expectations.text: behavioral_check: Explains that target_sampling_rate sets the downsampled output rate of LFPV1
expectations.evidence: Opening line states it is the rate LFPV1.make() will 'decimate the filtered LFP down to before storing it', and that it is the rate downstream consumers read back.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Connects the rate to the Nyquist constraint for downstream band filters (e.g., ripple band 150–250 Hz needs > 500 Hz sampling)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly walks through Nyquist for target=500 (Nyquist 250 Hz, ripple top edge), 400 Hz (Nyquist 200 Hz cuts off ripple), 300 Hz (Nyquist 150 Hz, below ripple band).

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Names 1000 Hz as the canonical default and the consequence of going lower (aliasing in the downstream band)
expectations.evidence: Names 1000 Hz as canonical default repeatedly, and details aliasing into the ripple band as the consequence of going lower.

expectations.text: behavioral_check: Does not say the sample rate is arbitrary or that any value works (this would contradict the Nyquist constraint)
expectations.evidence: Strongly conveys that rate is constrained by Nyquist and downstream filter cutoffs; explicitly calls arbitrary low rates a 'common mis-configuration'.
